claims.claim_text: A major e-commerce platform faced an AWS us-east-1 outage while processing $47,000 per minute.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.anchor_text: The on-call engineer at a major e-commerce platform checked her phone nervously. AWS had just posted to their status page: us-east-1 regional outage. Her company processed $47,000 per minute during peak hours.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A regional cloud outage can turn a manually operated failover plan into an expensive, high-stress incident for a high-volume platform.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed outage anecdote with fake-precise business metrics.

claims.claim_text: The previous outage took 8 minutes to detect.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Detection time | Several minutes passed before humans recognized the problem |
claims.rationale: Unsourced incident timing in a named outage table cannot be kept.

claims.claim_text: Manual failover coordination took 23 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Manual failover coordination | Manual failover added substantial operational delay |
claims.rationale: Unsourced incident timing in a named outage table cannot be kept.

claims.claim_text: DNS propagation to the DR site took 15 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.anchor_text: | DNS propagation to DR site | 15 minutes |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | DNS propagation to DR site | DNS cutover added more recovery delay |
claims.rationale: Unsourced incident timing in a named outage table cannot be kept.

claims.claim_text: Cache warming in us-west-2 took 12 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Cache warming in us-west-2 | Recovery also required warm-up time in the failover region |
claims.rationale: Unsourced incident timing in a named outage table cannot be kept.

claims.claim_text: Total downtime was 58 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | **Total downtime** | **Nearly an hour of disruption** |
claims.rationale: This precise outage duration is unsupported.

claims.claim_text: Revenue lost was $2,726,000.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | **Revenue lost** | **Substantial revenue was at risk during the outage** |
claims.rationale: This is fake-precise financial loss without a source.

claims.claim_text: SLA credits issued were $340,000.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | **SLA credits issued** | **Customer remediation costs followed the outage** |
claims.rationale: This is fake-precise financial impact without a source.

claims.claim_text: Customer churn over 90 days cost $1,134,000.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | **Customer churn (90 days)** | **Some longer-tail customer impact followed the incident** |
claims.rationale: This is fake-precise financial impact without a source.

claims.claim_text: Post-incident engineering required 120 hours costing $18,000.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters table
claims.suggested_rewrite: | **Post-incident engineering** | **Significant follow-up engineering work was required** |
claims.rationale: This is fake-precise labor costing without a source.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB handled the outage in 4.7 seconds with automatic leader election, zero human intervention, zero data loss, and zero customer impact.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.anchor_text: **CockroachDB handled the outage in 4.7 seconds—automatic leader election, zero human intervention, zero data loss, zero customer impact.**
claims.suggested_rewrite: **CockroachDB is designed so leadership can move automatically after failures, reducing or avoiding manual failover work when quorum is preserved.**
claims.rationale: This is a fabricated outage outcome with fake-precise timing and impact.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB uses the same Raft consensus algorithm used by etcd and Kubernetes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters
claims.rationale: The etcd Raft library README explicitly describes Raft and lists CockroachDB, etcd, and Kubernetes among systems it powers.

claims.claim_text: DoorDash's migration to CockroachDB saved an estimated $8M annually, moved 100+ microservices, and turned a 15-minute outage into a 50ms slowdown.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: - **DoorDash's migration to CockroachDB saved an estimated $8M annually** — They moved 100+ microservices from Aurora PostgreSQL to CockroachDB for multi-region active-active. Their "entire business offline" blast radius shrank to "single region latency increase"—a 15-minute outage became a 50ms slowdown.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **DoorDash has described moving important workloads from Aurora Postgres to CockroachDB** — The defensible lesson here is about reducing single-database blast radius and improving resilience, not a precise annual savings number.
claims.rationale: This mixes a real company with unsupported savings, scope, and incident numbers.

claims.claim_text: Cockroach Labs raised $633M, its Series F valued it at $5B, and the 2021 Facebook outage lost $60M in 6 hours.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: - **Cockroach Labs raised $633M on the promise of database resilience** — Their Series F valued the company at $5B. The pitch: every major database outage costs enterprises millions, and CockroachDB makes regional failures a non-event. The 2021 Facebook outage ($60M revenue lost in 6 hours) proved the point.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Cockroach Labs has raised significant funding around the argument that resilience matters** — Major outages can be extremely expensive for large businesses, but precise fundraising and outage-loss figures need direct sourcing.
claims.rationale: This combines multiple precise real-world claims without attributable sources.

claims.claim_text: A fintech cut DR costs from $2.1M/year to $340K by adopting CockroachDB active-active architecture.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: - **One fintech cut their DR costs from $2.1M/year to $340K** — Traditional disaster recovery meant maintaining a warm standby cluster that sat idle 99.99% of the time. CockroachDB's active-active architecture eliminated the need—all nodes serve traffic, all nodes are the DR site.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Some teams use active-active database designs to reduce the cost and complexity of warm-standby disaster recovery** — Exact savings depend on the workload, topology, and licensing model.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed case study with fake-precise savings.

claims.claim_text: A European bank avoided €847M in GDPR fines by using REGIONAL BY ROW to keep EU customer data in EU datacenters.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know
claims.anchor_text: - **GDPR compliance fines avoided: €847M in a single case study** — A European bank used `REGIONAL BY ROW` to guarantee EU customer data never leaves EU datacenters, even in a globally distributed cluster. Without this, they faced potential GDPR penalties of up to 4% of global revenue.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Features like `REGIONAL BY ROW` can help support data-locality and residency requirements** — Compliance outcomes still depend on the full system design and legal review, not just one database feature.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed compliance anecdote with fake-precise penalty figures.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB is a distributed SQL database built on a transactional, strongly consistent key-value store and designed for high availability and scale.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters / How CockroachDB Works
claims.rationale: The CockroachDB upstream repository README states these high-level capabilities directly.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB divides data into ranges, replicates them three ways by default, and uses Raft plus automatic splitting and rebalancing.
claims.span_hint: section: How CockroachDB Works / Ranges and Replication
claims.rationale: This is a real product-behavior claim, but the direct documentation is on cockroachlabs.com, which is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB offers SERIALIZABLE and READ COMMITTED isolation and defaults transactions to SERIALIZABLE.
claims.span_hint: section: How CockroachDB Works / Transaction Layer
claims.rationale: This is a real product-behavior claim, but the direct documentation is on cockroachlabs.com, which is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB can be deployed on Kubernetes with Helm and with an operator.
claims.span_hint: section: Installing CockroachDB on Kubernetes
claims.rationale: The product docs back this directly, but that host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB supports multi-region features such as SURVIVE REGION FAILURE and REGIONAL BY ROW for locality-aware placement.
claims.span_hint: section: Multi-Region Configuration
claims.rationale: The direct product documentation is off allowlist even though the claim is real.

claims.claim_text: The module recommends PgBouncer or another connection pool in front of CockroachDB.
claims.span_hint: section: Common Mistakes
claims.anchor_text: | No connection pooling | Connection overhead at scale | Use PgBouncer or connection pool |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | No connection pooling | Connection overhead at scale | Use a connection-pooling layer appropriate for your stack |
claims.rationale: The general lesson is sound, but the exact PgBouncer recommendation is not directly sourced here.

claims.claim_text: The module recommends 8+ cores and 32GB+ RAM per CockroachDB node.
claims.span_hint: section: Common Mistakes
claims.anchor_text: | Undersized nodes | CPU/memory bottlenecks | 8+ cores, 32GB+ RAM per node |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Undersized nodes | CPU/memory bottlenecks | Size nodes from workload measurements and current vendor guidance |
claims.rationale: This exact sizing rule is too precise to keep without a direct source.

claims.claim_text: The closing pull-quote about CockroachDB treating disasters as normal events is presented as a quotation without attribution.
claims.span_hint: section: closing epigraph
claims.anchor_text: *"The best disaster recovery is a system that doesn't consider disasters special. CockroachDB treats node failures, network partitions, and even regional outages as normal events to handle automatically."*
claims.suggested_rewrite: CockroachDB is designed to handle routine failures automatically when the cluster still has the quorum and topology it needs.
claims.rationale: An unattributed verbatim-style quote should be rewritten into ordinary prose.

claims.claim_text: In 2021 Aurora PostgreSQL maxed out at 10K TPS and the site went read-only for 2 hours.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / The Setup
claims.anchor_text: - 2021: Aurora PostgreSQL maxed out at 10K TPS, site went read-only for 2 hours
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Earlier peak events exposed scaling limits in the prior Aurora PostgreSQL design.
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported historical outage claim with precise throughput and duration.

claims.claim_text: In 2022 manual failover caused 45 minutes of degraded service and $1.2M in lost sales.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / The Setup
claims.anchor_text: - 2022: Manual failover during peak load, 45 minutes of degraded service, $1.2M in lost sales
claims.suggested_rewrite: - A previous peak-season incident still required manual failover and caused customer-visible degradation.
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported historical outage claim with fake-precise impact numbers.

claims.claim_text: At 12:45 PM EST a us-east-1a availability-zone network issue occurred during peak traffic.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / The Test
claims.anchor_text: - us-east-1a availability zone experiences network issues
claims.suggested_rewrite: - A peak-period availability-zone network issue briefly disrupted part of the deployment
claims.rationale: This is a real-event style incident claim without a source.

claims.claim_text: Three CockroachDB nodes in that availability zone became temporarily unreachable.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / The Test
claims.anchor_text: - 3 CockroachDB nodes in that AZ become temporarily unreachable
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Several CockroachDB nodes in the affected zone became temporarily unreachable
claims.rationale: This is a real-event style incident claim without a source.

claims.claim_text: New leaders were elected within 5 seconds in us-east-1b and us-east-1c.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / What happened
claims.anchor_text: 2. Within 5 seconds, new leaders elected in us-east-1b and us-east-1c
claims.suggested_rewrite: 2. New leaders were elected automatically on surviving replicas after the failure

claims.suggested_rewrite: 3. Write latency increased noticeably for the affected ranges during failover

claims.claim_text: There were zero failed transactions because clients retried automatically.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / What happened
claims.suggested_rewrite: 4. Application retries can hide some failover turbulence, but exact transaction outcomes need real evidence

claims.claim_text: There was zero data loss.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / What happened
claims.suggested_rewrite: 5. Whether data loss occurs depends on quorum, replication, and the exact failure mode
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported absolute incident outcome.

claims.claim_text: Raft replication caught up within 30 seconds after recovery.

claims.anchor_text: - Raft replication caught up within 30 seconds
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Replication caught up shortly after the affected nodes returned
claims.rationale: This is fake-precise recovery timing without a source.

claims.claim_text: The CFO said the migration paid for itself in a single Black Friday and seven times over.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / Financial Impact
claims.anchor_text: The CFO's comment in the board meeting: "The CockroachDB migration paid for itself in a single Black Friday. Actually, it paid for itself 7 times over. And next year there's no migration cost—it's pure upside."
claims.suggested_rewrite: A resilience migration can have a strong business case, but it should be argued with a real postmortem and a finance model rather than an invented boardroom quote.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed verbatim-style quote plus unsupported ROI claim.

further_reading.title: CockroachDB Upstream Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for high-level architecture claims and the project's own description of CockroachDB.

further_reading.title: CockroachDB Kubernetes Operator
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for Kubernetes deployment details and operator-managed cluster behavior.

further_reading.title: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for Raft, the consensus algorithm discussed in the module.

notes: Excluded fenced code blocks, quiz items, and exercise steps per the brief. The shared section pool was only tangentially relevant here; most direct CockroachDB product documentation lives on cockroachlabs.com, so legitimate product-specific claims are often marked needs_allowlist_expansion.
